title: drosera longifolia var. vulgaris
intro: Drosera longifolia var. vulgaris, commonly known as the long-leaved sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. The plant's leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Drosera longifolia var. vulgaris include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate sinus congestion. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
